authority_model: ph-civ is the upstream public artifact and source of truth; ph-civ-zh and ph-civ-ru are downstream localization mirrors that inherit route IDs, guardrails, cards, transcripts, commentaries, and launch-readiness status from ph-civ.
identity: ph-civ is an LLM-native trilingual civilizational bridge: English, Chinese, and Russian readerships can reinforce each other by reading the Western canon, historical pattern, and modern crisis through a shared, source-bounded public route system.
english_hook: A Chinese civilizational-history lens can help Western readers recover the stakes of their own canon without turning that canon into nostalgia or culture-war shorthand.
chinese_hook: The Western canon can become readable as civilizational pattern without imitation or rejection, and without civilizational scoreboard thinking.
reinforcement_loop: English readers share the surprise that a Chinese civilizational-history lens can clarify the Western canon.
Chinese readers share the confidence that Western classics can be studied as historical pattern systems, not merely consumed as prestige objects.
Russian readers add a third reinforcement line through the Homer-to-Tolstoy spine and Russian literary depth.
Cross-language attention creates social proof: each readership sees the others reading seriously.
Trilingual LLM chats make the route system portable across English, Chinese, and Russian contexts.
Route, commentary, and museum refinements compound as readers test the same source-bounded IDs from both directions.
guardrails: not an independent source of truth
not a replacement for source cards, transcripts, commentary, and evidence limits

future_zh_wedge.defer: Do not translate all 149 source chapters before the glossary, bootloader, and first-tour metadata are stable.

future_ru_wedge.reader_hook: Russian readers can enter the Homer-to-Tolstoy spine through shared route IDs, Russian literary depth, and civilizational pattern study without turning the surface into present-war apologetics.
future_ru_wedge.defer: Do not translate all 149 source chapters or create ph-civ-ru commands before the glossary, bootloader, first-tour metadata, and Russia-specific guardrails are stable.

public_copy.short: Homer to Tolstoy, read from China.
public_copy.long: A trilingual civilizational bridge where canonical English ph-civ can support downstream Chinese and Russian mirrors through the same source-bounded route system, without propaganda, flattery, loose translation, or independent localization canons.
